channel.title: The Athletic - Ohio State Buckeyes (College Football)
channel.description: Ohio State Buckeyes college football news from The Athletic (NYTimes).

item.title: Submit a question for Stewart Mandel's College Football Mailbag
item.description: The Brendan Sorsby saga continues to dominate the news, but there’s plenty more college football to discuss. Leave your questions here. Please include your city.

item.title: Big Ten schools receive record $1.37 billion from 2024-25 fiscal year
item.description: The 2024-25 fiscal year was the Big Ten’s first with 18 members and coincided with a new media rights deal and the new 12-team CFP.

item.title: Is QB Julian Sayin a first-round talent? Was OL coach Tyler Bowen a bad hire? Ohio State Mailbag
item.description: Cameron Teague Robinson digs into the Buckeyes football mailbag, including a question about quarterback Julian Sayin's NFL Draft prospects.

item.title: College football transfer portal DL rankings: Damon Wilson II, Princewill Umanmielen top list
item.description: Seven of the top 20 D-line transfers are headed to the SEC, six to the Big 12, four to the Big Ten, two to Notre Dame and one to the ACC.

item.title: Brendan Sorsby and the transfer portal's vetting problem; is the 2027 QB class overhyped? Mandel's Mailbag
item.description: With how fast the transfer portal moves, how much can schools really find out about players they're paying millions?

item.title: Submit a question for Stewart Mandel's College Football Mailbag
item.description: There was some big news out of Texas Tech on Monday, and this week’s Mailbag is going to run a day later than usual on Thursday. What else might come out? Leave your questions here. Please include your city.

item.title: Submit your Ohio State football questions for Cameron Teague Robinson’s mailbag
item.description: The spring game is over. The roster is set. What’s on your mind? Submit your Ohio State football questions below.

item.title: How prepared is Ohio State to replace another 11 NFL Draft picks? What we've learned so far
item.description: The Buckeyes have led all colleges in NFL Draft picks each of the past two years. Here's how their 2026 roster is coming together.

item.title: Buffalo Bills draft Ohio State cornerback Davison Igbinosun with 62nd pick in 2026 NFL Draft
item.description: Igbinosun was an outside boundary cornerback and three-year starter for the Buckeyes.

item.title: Los Angeles Rams select Ohio State TE Max Klare with 61st pick of 2026 NFL Draft
item.description: After three seasons at Purdue, tight end Max Klare totaled 43 receptions and 448 receiving yards for Ohio State in 2025.

item.title: Texans select Ohio State defensive tackle Kayden McDonald with No. 36 pick in 2026 NFL Draft
item.description: One of four new starters on Ohio State's defensive line in 2025, McDonald broke as out the Big Ten's defensive lineman of the year.

item.title: Big night for the Big Ten: 2026 NFL Draft thoughts through a college football lens
item.description: The Big Ten had the best team in college football in 2025 — and also had the most players drafted in the first round.

item.title: College football transfer portal OL rankings: LSU's Jordan Seaton tops the list
item.description: Twelve of the top 15 players on the list will play in the SEC next season, including three who signed with Kentucky.

item.title: Giants got better on draft night, but the two most essential picks remain Harbaugh, Dart
item.description: It was no surprise that, after selecting edge rusher Arvell Reese at No. 5, John Harbaugh and Joe Schoen did something kind for their QB.

item.title: Dallas Cowboys select Ohio State safety Caleb Downs with No. 11 pick in 2026 NFL Draft
item.description: Caleb Downs' versatility was the centerpiece of Ohio State's juggernaut defense this past season.

item.title: Washington Commanders select LB Sonny Styles with No. 7 pick in 2026 NFL Draft
item.description: Styles flourished at linebacker the past two years after starting his Ohio State career as a safety.

item.title: Ohio State's Arvell Reese selected by New York Giants with No. 5 pick in 2026 NFL Draft
item.description: Reese started for only one season, but he broke out as an All-American and freakish athlete off the edge for an elite Ohio State defense.

item.title: Carnell Tate, WR from Ohio State, selected by Titans with No. 4 pick in 2026 NFL Draft
item.description: Tate is the sixth Ohio State wide receiver picked in the first round of the NFL Draft since 2022.

item.title: How Commanders pick Sonny Styles unlocked top-10 draft potential after a position switch
item.description: How Styles developed into a top-10 pick of the Washington Commanders after converting from safety to linebacker at Ohio State.

item.title: How did David Bailey end up at Stanford? Recruiting backstories of top NFL Draft prospects
item.description: Two of the top players on the board almost ended up in the Ivy League, while several others were coveted by every school in the nation.

item.title: Should Michigan be nervous about Bryce Underwood? Do athletes still go to class? Mandel's Mailbag
item.description: Plus, with Week 0 start, could we have two weeks straight of games? Which schools get to claim they put a player in the NFL?

item.title: Submit a question for Stewart Mandel's College Football Mailbag
item.description: We’re about halfway through spring game season (or at least what’s left of it). What’s on your mind? Leave your questions here. Please include your city.

item.title: Ohio State spring football takeaways: Julian Sayin grows, Tavien St. Clair shines
item.description: The defense won 35-26, a flip from the offense’s dominance a year ago, and 51 new players saw their first playing time in front of fans.

item.title: What will we learn from Ohio State's spring game? A QB showcase, new faces and more
item.description: With so many new players and changes to the program, there are plenty of storylines to talk about as Ohio State holds its spring scrimmage.

item.title: Big Ten football survey: How do you feel about the conference amid its current hot streak?
item.description: Times are good in Big Ten country. Will they continue, or is the success cyclical? We want to hear your thoughts.
